title: tetragonolobus requienii
intro: Tetragonolobus requienii, also known as the horse bean or Italian bean, is a leguminous plant native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its small, white or pinkish flowers and long, slender pods containing several seeds. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antispasmodic, anti-inflammatory, and diuretic effects. The health benefits of Tetragonolobus requienii include relief from menstrual cramps, digestive issues, and urinary tract problems, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health. In herbal preparations, the plant's seeds are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
